2:T785,{"@context":"https://schema.org","@type":"FAQPage","mainEntity":[{"@type":"Question","name":"What is an error code?","acceptedAnswer":{"@type":"Answer","text":"An error code is a diagnostic code displayed by a device (appliance, vehicle, or system) to indicate a specific malfunction or fault. These codes help technicians identify the problem quickly."}},{"@type":"Question","name":"How do I find the error code on my appliance?","acceptedAnswer":{"@type":"Answer","text":"Error codes are typically displayed on the device's control panel or digital display. Check your user manual for the specific location. Some devices require entering diagnostic mode to view codes."}},{"@type":"Question","name":"What does F21 mean on a Whirlpool washer?","acceptedAnswer":{"@type":"Answer","text":"F21 indicates a drain timeout error - the washer couldn't drain water within 8 minutes. Common causes: clogged drain hose, faulty pump, or blocked filter. Solution: clean drain hose and pump filter."}},{"@type":"Question","name":"What does P0300 mean on a car?","acceptedAnswer":{"@type":"Answer","text":"P0300 is a random/multiple cylinder misfire code. It means the engine is misfiring in multiple cylinders randomly. Common causes: worn spark plugs, faulty ignition coils, or fuel injector issues."}},{"@type":"Question","name":"What does P0AA6 mean on an electric vehicle?","acceptedAnswer":{"@type":"Answer","text":"P0AA6 indicates a hybrid battery voltage isolation fault - the high-voltage battery system has detected an isolation problem. This is a safety-critical fault that requires immediate professional attention."}},{"@type":"Question","name":"How accurate are these error codes?","acceptedAnswer":{"@type":"Answer","text":"Our error codes are sourced from manufacturer service manuals, OBD-II standards (SAE J2012), and verified by professional technicians. Each code includes multiple potential causes and solutions."}}]}0:["x1492lauvCPRllH83A_ES",[[["",{"children":["faq",{"children":["__PAGE__",{}]}]},"$undefined","$undefined",true],["",{"children":["faq",{"children":["__PAGE__",{},[["$L1",["$","div",null,{"style":{"maxWidth":"800px","margin":"0 auto","padding":"40px 20px"},"children":[["$","script",null,{"type":"application/ld+json","dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"$2"}}],["$","h1",null,{"style":{"fontSize":"2.5rem","fontWeight":"bold","marginBottom":"10px","color":"white"},"children":"❓ Frequently Asked Questions"}],["$","p",null,{"style":{"color":"#94a3b8","marginBottom":"40px","fontSize":"1.1rem"},"children":"Expert answers to common questions about error codes • Updated 2026-03-18"}],["$","div",null,{"style":{"display":"flex","flexDirection":"column","gap":"16px"},"children":[["$","details","0",{"style":{"background":"#1e293b","borderRadius":"12px","padding":"20px","cursor":"pointer","border":"1px solid #334155"},"children":[["$","summary",null,{"style":{"fontWeight":"600","fontSize":"1.1rem","color":"white","listStyle":"none"},"children":"What is an error code?"}],["$","p",null,{"style":{"color":"#94a3b8","marginTop":"12px","lineHeight":"1.7","fontSize":"1rem"},"children":"An error code is a diagnostic code displayed by a device (appliance, vehicle, or system) to indicate a specific malfunction or fault. Solution: clean drain hose and pump filter."}]]}],["$","details","3",{"style":{"background":"#1e293b","borderRadius":"12px","padding":"20px","cursor":"pointer","border":"1px solid #334155"},"children":[["$","summary",null,{"style":{"fontWeight":"600","fontSize":"1.1rem","color":"white","listStyle":"none"},"children":"What does P0300 mean on a car?"}],["$","p",null,{"style":{"color":"#94a3b8","marginTop":"12px","lineHeight":"1.7","fontSize":"1rem"},"children":"P0300 is a random/multiple cylinder misfire code. It means the engine is misfiring in multiple cylinders randomly. Common causes: worn spark plugs, faulty ignition coils, or fuel injector issues."}]]}],["$","details","4",{"style":{"background":"#1e293b","borderRadius":"12px","padding":"20px","cursor":"pointer","border":"1px solid #334155"},"children":[["$","summary",null,{"style":{"fontWeight":"600","fontSize":"1.1rem","color":"white","listStyle":"none"},"children":"What does P0AA6 mean on an electric vehicle?"}],["$","p",null,{"style":{"color":"#94a3b8","marginTop":"12px","lineHeight":"1.7","fontSize":"1rem"},"children":"P0AA6 indicates a hybrid battery voltage isolation fault - the high-voltage battery system has detected an isolation problem. This is a safety-critical fault that requires immediate professional attention."}]]}],["$","details","5",{"style":{"background":"#1e293b","borderRadius":"12px","padding":"20px","cursor":"pointer","border":"1px solid #334155"},"children":[["$","summary",null,{"style":{"fontWeight":"600","fontSize":"1.1rem","color":"white","listStyle":"none"},"children":"How accurate are these error codes?"}],["$","p",null,{"style":{"color":"#94a3b8","marginTop":"12px","lineHeight":"1.7","fontSize":"1rem"},"children":"Our error codes are sourced from manufacturer service manuals, OBD-II standards (SAE J2012), and verified by professional technicians. Each code includes multiple potential causes and solutions."}]]}],["$","details","6",{"style":{"background":"#1e293b","borderRadius":"12px","padding":"20px","cursor":"pointer","border":"1px solid #334155"},"children":[["$","summary",null,{"style":{"fontWeight":"600","fontSize":"1.1rem","color":"white","listStyle":"none"},"children":"Can I fix the error code myself?"}],["$","p",null,{"style":{"color":"#94a3b8","marginTop":"12px","lineHeight":"1.7","fontSize":"1rem"},"children":"Many error codes indicate simple issues that can be fixed DIY-style (like cleaning a filter or resetting the device). However, electrical or mechanical issues should be handled by professionals."}]]}],["$","details","7",{"style":{"background":"#1e293b","borderRadius":"12px","padding":"20px","cursor":"pointer","border":"1px solid #334155"},"children":[["$","summary",null,{"style":{"fontWeight":"600","fontSize":"1.1rem","color":"white","listStyle":"none"},"children":"Why do different brands use different error codes?"}],["$","p",null,{"style":{"color":"#94a3b8","marginTop":"12px","lineHeight":"1.7","fontSize":"1rem"},"children":"Each manufacturer develops their own diagnostic systems. However, automotive OBD-II codes are standardized (P0xxx for powertrain). Appliance codes vary by manufacturer but often follow similar patterns."}]]}],["$","details","8",{"style":{"background":"#1e293b","borderRadius":"12px","padding":"20px","cursor":"pointer","border":"1px solid #334155"},"children":[["$","summary",null,{"style":{"fontWeight":"600","fontSize":"1.1rem","color":"white","listStyle":"none"},"children":"What tools do I need to diagnose error codes?"}],["$","p",null,{"style":{"color":"#94a3b8","marginTop":"12px","lineHeight":"1.7","fontSize":"1rem"},"children":"Basic: user manual and smartphone. Professional: OBD-II scanner (vehicles), multimeter, and manufacturer-specific diagnostic tools for appliances."}]]}],["$","details","9",{"style":{"background":"#1e293b","borderRadius":"12px","padding":"20px","cursor":"pointer","border":"1px solid #334155"},"children":[["$","summary",null,{"style":{"fontWeight":"600","fontSize":"1.1rem","color":"white","listStyle":"none"},"children":"Can error codes be cleared without repair?"}],["$","p",null,{"style":{"color":"#94a3b8","marginTop":"12px","lineHeight":"1.7","fontSize":"1rem"},"children":"Clearing codes without fixing the underlying issue is not recommended - the problem will likely return. However, a temporary clear may help identify if the issue is intermittent."}]]}],["$","details","10",{"style":{"background":"#1e293b","borderRadius":"12px","padding":"20px","cursor":"pointer","border":"1px solid #334155"},"children":[["$","summary",null,{"style":{"fontWeight":"600","fontSize":"1.1rem","color":"white","listStyle":"none"},"children":"How often should I update my error code database?"}],["$","p",null,{"style":{"color":"#94a3b8","marginTop":"12px","lineHeight":"1.7","fontSize":"1rem"},"children":"We update our database weekly with new codes from latest models and corrections. Vehicle manufacturers release new codes annually with new model years."}]]}],["$","details","11",{"style":{"background":"#1e293b","borderRadius":"12px","padding":"20px","cursor":"pointer","border":"1px solid #334155"},"children":[["$","summary",null,{"style":{"fontWeight":"600","fontSize":"1.1rem","color":"white","listStyle":"none"},"children":"What does OE mean on a washing machine?"}],["$","p",null,{"style":{"color":"#94a3b8","marginTop":"12px","lineHeight":"1.7","fontSize":"1rem"},"children":"OE typically indicates an overflow error or drain issue. This means water has exceeded the safe level. Turn off the machine immediately and check the drain hose and pressure switch."}]]}]]}],["$","div",null,{"style":{"marginTop":"40px","padding":"24px","background":"linear-gradient(135deg, #1e293b 0%,
